articles.title: ‘We hit Iran twice, will do it again if...’: Netanyahu issues fresh warning over nuclear programme
articles.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that Israel could strike Iran again, saying the country has acted twice before and would not hesitate to launch a third operation to stop Tehran from acquiring nuclear weapons.

articles.description: India's Got Latent: Abhijeet Dipke says he will never appear on Samay Raina's show, calling the comedian privileged. His remarks sparked debate online, with critics pointing to his Boston education as the latest controversy around the series.

articles.title: Did You Know The World's First Cosmetic Surgeon Came From Ancient India?
articles.description: National Doctors' Day 2026: From nose reconstruction to advanced surgical training, this Indian physician was centuries ahead of his time.

articles.title: Delhi May Soon Get Its Own London Eye As DDA Revives Giant Observation Wheel Project
articles.description: Delhi could soon have its own iconic observation wheel with the proposed Delhi Eye project in Mayur Vihar. Planned by the Delhi Development Authority (DDA), the 35-metre-tall attraction aims to become a major tourism and recreational landmark in East Delhi. The project will be developed through a public-private partnership, with private firms invited to build, operate and maintain the facility while the land remains with the DDA. If completed, the Delhi Eye could offer panoramic views of the capital and transform Mayur Vihar into a new tourism hotspot.

articles.title: Beyond surveillance: humanitarian lessons on integrating physical health into refugee health responses
articles.description: Agreement: I Agree Body: Dear Editor, The BMJ article by Touhid powerfully illustrates how dengue exposes the fragility of health systems serving displaced populations. While the immediate challenge is vector-borne disease, the wider lesson extends beyond Bangladesh. Humanitarian responses must resist separating infectious disease control from broader physical healthcare and communication support. During the UK Afghan resettlement programme following the fall of Kabul in 2021, we observed a similar principle in a very different setting. Although our role primarily focused on mental health and safeguarding assessments, many newly arrived families presented with untreated physical health needs that had remained unrecognised throughout displacement, including poorly controlled chronic diseases, dental pathology, anaemia, pregnancy-related complications, and unmet disability needs. These conditions were not unusual; they reflected disrupted healthcare access and the realities of forced migration. The Bangladesh experience highlights how infectious diseases flourish where health systems are overwhelmed, surveillance is incomplete, and displaced communities face barriers to care. Refugee camps in Cox's Bazar present obvious environmental risks for dengue transmission, but the exclusion of camp data from national surveillance also demonstrates how health inequalities can become statistically invisible. Similar invisibility occurs in high-income countries when refugee health is viewed solely through a mental health or safeguarding lens, while physical health needs rely on individuals successfully navigating unfamiliar healthcare systems. Communication is central to both contexts. Early recognition of deteriorating illness—whether dengue shock syndrome or pregnancy complications—depends on timely access to healthcare, professional interpretation, culturally appropriate health information, and trust in health services. These are not optional additions but core patient safety interventions. Delays in seeking care should not be understood simply as individual decisions but as consequences of structural, linguistic, and systemic barriers. Humanitarian medicine has long recognised that physical health, mental health, and social circumstances are inseparable. Initial health assessments routinely incorporate infectious disease, chronic illness, nutrition, pregnancy, disability, and psychosocial needs within a single framework. As global displacement increases and climate change alters the epidemiology of infectious diseases, this integrated approach becomes increasingly relevant beyond humanitarian settings. The authors rightly emphasise the interaction between climate change, displacement, and dengue. Health systems must now prepare not only for emerging infectious diseases but also for increasingly mobile populations whose healthcare has often been interrupted long before arrival. Strengthening surveillance is essential, but so too is ensuring that displaced populations receive holistic, accessible, and communication-aware healthcare from first contact. Bangladesh's experience reminds us that the consequences of delayed recognition are measured not only in epidemiological data, but also in preventable suffering and avoidable deaths. Humanitarian health responses offer valuable lessons for health systems worldwide: comprehensive screening, equitable communication, and integrated physical and mental healthcare should be regarded as fundamental components of preparedness rather than emergency measures. No competing Interests: Yes The following competing Interests: Electronic Publication Date: Wednesday, July 1, 2026 - 05:44 AI use: Yes I have used AI Highwire Comment Subject: Dengue poses an increasingly severe threat to Bangladesh—and the world’s largest refugee camp AI use details: No AI tools were used to generate the content, ideas, analysis, or authorship of this article. AI-assisted software was used solely for minor grammar and spelling checks. articles.title: Days after Pakistan's airstrikes, Afghanistan launches strikes on ISIS targets in Balochistan
articles.description: Afghanistan's Taliban forces conducted strikes in Pakistan's Balochistan province, targeting alleged Islamic State bases. This follows Pakistan's recent airstrikes in Afghanistan, which killed civilians and were condemned by India as a "blatant act of aggression." Tensions are escalating after a period of relative calm, with intermittent border clashes and air strikes claiming dozens of lives recently.

articles.title: Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports
articles.description: Indian auto manufacturers celebrated robust June sales, fueled by strong domestic demand and surging exports. Maruti Suzuki reported over 200,000 units sold, while Mahindra & Mahindra saw a significant 37% growth with over 106,000 vehicles. Hyundai Motor India also posted strong figures, overcoming a temporary production setback due to a supplier issue. These impressive numbers highlight a thriving automotive sector.

articles.title: Apple iPhone Ultra vs Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide: What we know and what to expect
articles.description: Apple's first foldable iPhone is expected to take on Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide, but the two devices will likely target different audiences.

articles.title: Trading Plan: Will Nifty 50, Bank Nifty snap their two-day losing streak on the first day of July series amid range-bound trading?
articles.description: In case of a bounce-back, the Nifty 50 is likely to face resistance in the 24,000-24,100 zone. However, support is placed at 23,800, as a fall below this level could bring the bears to the forefront. Overall, the trading range for the index remains at 23,800-24,200 going ahead.

articles.description: The FIFA World Cup Round of 32 match between Mexico and Ecuador at Estadio Azteca was postponed due to adverse weather. Co-hosts Mexico enter with a perfect group stage record, while Ecuador squeezed through as a third-placed team.

articles.description: Egypt captain Mohamed Salah has returned to partial training after a hamstring injury. This raises hopes he could be fit for the World Cup Round of 32 clash against Australia in Dallas, providing a major boost for the team.

articles.title: Gruha Jyothi Yojana: Karnataka launches door-to-door verification
articles.description: The Karnataka Energy Department began a door-to-door verification drive for Gruha Jyothi Yojana beneficiaries. The drive aims to update records for the free electricity scheme. Beneficiaries are asked to keep documents ready for ESCOM staff.

articles.title: Erling Haaland scores in 13th straight game, sets historic WC record
articles.description: Erling Haaland scored a late winner as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 to advance to the Round of 16. Haaland became the first player in 72 years to score in his first 3 World Cup starts, extending his scoring streak to 13 consecutive games.

articles.title: CSIR labs' visibility enhanced under PM Modi, says Jitendra Singh
articles.description: Union Minister Jitendra Singh inaugurated new facilities at CSIR-IICT Hyderabad, including an Effluent Treatment Plant. He praised PM Modi for boosting the visibility of CSIR labs and called for stronger industry-innovation ties for agri-startups.

articles.title: India's UPI goes live in Greece with Eurobank, Piyush Goyal attends
articles.description: Union Minister Piyush Goyal witnessed the launch of India's UPI services at Eurobank headquarters in Athens. The partnership enables instant, secure, and low-cost money transfers, marking a significant milestone in India's global digital expansion.

articles.title: As Canada's 'Palantir' creates new funding record; CEO says: Ambition is to change ...
articles.description: Canadian defence tech firm Dominion Dynamics secured a record C$139 million Series A funding, aiming to revitalise the nation's defence capabilities. CEO Eliot Pence envisions building a major Canadian defence prime, focusing on Arctic-hardened surveillance and autonomous platforms. This investment fuels development of their AuraNet software and Scout autonomous system, bolstering Canada's defence industrial strategy amidst rising global military spending.

articles.title: GCC councillor moves Madras High Court against Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's birthday celebrations in schools
articles.description: A Greater Chennai Corporation councillor has approached the Madras High Court, challenging the alleged use of educational institutions for political glorification, specifically questioning the inclusion of students in Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay's birthday celebrations. Reports detail an event at a Namakkal school where students faced hardship, including prolonged waits in the sun and manual labor, prompting criticism and an apology from an organizer

articles.description: Supreme Court footpath ruling has put the spotlight on Pune's pedestrian infrastructure as the PMC expands footpath construction, repairs and monitoring across the city.

articles.title: MDMK general secretary Vaiko loses cool over DMK alliance question; supporters try to attack reporters
articles.description: MDMK leader Vaiko lost his composure at a press meet when questioned about his criticism of the DMK. He alleged widespread corruption during the DMK's tenure, particularly in land registration. When pressed on why he hadn't raised these issues earlier, Vaiko cited coalition ethics. The situation escalated with his supporters attempting to confront reporters, but police intervention diffused the tension.

articles.title: Huge fire in naphtha pipeline in Bengal's Haldia, many injured: watch what happened| East Midnapore
articles.description: At least 20 people were injured, including six critically, after a major fire broke out in a naphtha pipeline at Haldia Petrochemicals Limited in East Midnapore district, West Bengal, early on June 30. The blaze erupted around 5 a.m., prompting the deployment of nearly 10 fire tenders to control the flames. Several shanties located near the pipeline were gutted in the fire. According to Haldia Petrochemicals Limited, preliminary findings suggest the incident may have occurred near an unauthorized naphtha theft point. The company said naphtha is a highly combustible and extremely flammable hydrocarbon and reiterated its warnings against illegal access to petroleum products. Authorities have launched an investigation to determine the exact cause of the fire.

articles.title: From OTT revival to franchise success: ‘Bharatanatyam 3: Rasaleela’ set for 2028 release
articles.description: Saiju Kurup’s Bharatanatyam franchise expands with the announcement of the third film Rasaleela after the success of Mohiniyattam, continuing the popular Malayalam film series.

articles.title: Extortion syndicates based abroad behind firing incidents in Maharashtra: CM Fadnavis
articles.description: Organized extortion syndicates operating from abroad are orchestrating firing incidents in Maharashtra, Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis revealed. These groups use VPNs and virtual numbers to threaten individuals and hire local criminals for attacks when demands are not met. The government is working with the Centre to apprehend those involved and dismantle these criminal networks, including those linked to gangster Lawrence Bishnoi.

articles.title: Class 9 Girl Survives Petrol Attack In Bhadrak; Accused Detained
articles.description: Bhadrak: A Class 9 schoolgirl survived a petrol attack in the Lanjara Chowk area under Fatepur Panchayat in the Dhusuri police station limits of Bhadrak

articles.title: Why Indian women abroad are investing more from the West than the Gulf
articles.description: Indian women living overseas are participating in cross-border investing in record numbers, with female investor participation rising nearly 70% over the past year. However, the trend is far more pronounced in Western countries than in the Gulf, highlighting how migration patterns and financial independence shape investment behaviour.

articles.title: 'If you spend all day in a fish market, you’ll carry the smell of fish': Harsh Goenka shares an important life lesson that can change how you see opportunities
articles.description: Industrialist Harsh Goenka shared a powerful social media message highlighting how our daily environments profoundly shape our perspectives. He illustrated this by comparing exposure to a fish market versus a perfume shop, and the company of complainers versus grateful individuals. Goenka's insight emphasizes that our surroundings can either foster a focus on problems or cultivate an outlook of endless possibilities, urging a conscious choice in our daily associations.

articles.title: Pune Inc | How gourmand Karen Anand’s curated trips go beyond traditional tourism
articles.description: 'Travel with Karen's' groups are made up of people who enjoy meeting local influencers and intellectuals, historical and cultural experts, and come away “ignited and delighted”.

articles.description: | After its repeated attempts to internationalise developments in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) failed to gain traction, Pakistan’s civil-military establishment is now preparing to launch a fresh global campaign centred on the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), according to top intelligence sources. n18oc_breaking-news News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.title: ‘You were our lost... ’: Supriya Pathak’s words for Mira Kapoor throw light on the kind of relationship she shares with her daughter-in-law: 3 takeaways for women
articles.description: Veteran actress Supriya Pathak shares a heartwarming bond with her daughter-in-law, Mira Kapoor, emphasizing genuine acceptance over traditional roles. Pathak views Mira not as a daughter-in-law, but as a beloved daughter who has always belonged. This relationship, built on emotional safety and mutual respect, offers a refreshing contrast to common in-law dynamics, highlighting how warmth and openness foster deep family connections.
